items.question: The canonical idea behind machine learning is:
options: Encoding every business rule by hand in source code
Learning patterns from data to improve task performance
Searching a fixed database with SQL queries
Running the same static formula on all inputs forever
items.answer: Learning patterns from data to improve task performance
items.explanation: ML systems fit parameters from examples (experience) so performance on a task improves without exhaustive manual rules.

items.question: In Mitchell's framing, a program learns when:
options: It uses more CPU cores
Performance on task T measured by P improves with experience E
The codebase has more lines
It stores all training rows in a lookup table
items.answer: Performance on task T measured by P improves with experience E
items.explanation: Learning is defined by measurable improvement on a task as experience (data) grows—not by hardware or memorization alone.

items.question: Training differs from inference because training:
options: Updates model parameters using data
Never sees labeled examples
Only runs on production traffic
Requires no dataset
items.explanation: Training fits parameters; inference applies the trained model to new inputs without further parameter updates.

items.question: Which situation is the best fit for machine learning?
options: Overtime pay = 1.5x hourly rate after 40 hours (fixed labor law)
Detecting fraudulent credit transactions from millions of past labels
Sorting a list of integers with a known O(n log n) algorithm
Converting Celsius to Fahrenheit with F = 9/5*C + 32
items.answer: Detecting fraudulent credit transactions from millions of past labels
items.explanation: Fraud patterns are complex and evolving; ML learns from labeled transaction history. Fixed formulas and known algorithms do not require learning from data.

items.question: Generalization means:
options: Perfect accuracy on training data only
Performing well on new data not used during training
Using the largest possible neural network
Collecting data without labels
items.answer: Performing well on new data not used during training
items.explanation: A useful ML model captures patterns that transfer to unseen examples; memorizing train rows is not generalization.
